_meta.purpose: Extracted from QC_Tasks/V3_Tasks/Task11..Task14 Rubrics.json. Defines the only phrasings, verb shapes, and qualifier rules considered on-framework. Anything in a deliverable that does not match these patterns is a Major convention drift â€” Council A blocks it.
_meta.note: All 59 rubrics across the 4 V3 reference tasks are outcome. Process is theoretically allowed but practically vanishingly rare. Default to zero process rubrics.

outcome_1_1_write_action: adds a status comment to Linear issue
outcome_2_1_key_facts: Agent reports in the final response

never_for: exact static dollar figures from a single record
examples_seen: approximately $435.41 (NOTE: not flagged â€” accepted as calculated rounded)

or_similar.placement: End of the phrase being relaxed, in parentheses
never_for: JE / exception / vendor / doc / issue ids
exact dollar amounts pulled from a single record
examples_seen: subject related to a relocation proposal (or similar)
noting that the reissue is absent and the original invoice remains blocked (or similar statement)
or similar statement that the reissue is absent

at_least_N.rule: Banned in rubric titles unless the prompt explicitly mandates a minimum count using 'at least N' phrasing.
at_least_N.alternative: Write one atomic rubric per ground-truth item.
at_least_N.examples_seen: None in V3 reference tasks (zero hits).

slack_rubrics: whether the post is channel-level vs thread reply when material

evidence_field_patterns: Look for a <verb>-... tool call in the trajectory with the <param> field set to <value>.
Look for a <kind> call ... Confirm the tool returned a success response.
Check the <param> parameter of the <tool> call for <pattern>.
Check the body / text field of the <tool> call targeting <id>. The comment should reference <X> or <Y> (or similar).
Check the agent's final response for <statement>.
Look for ... and verify ... and that the tool returned a success response.
Check the content parameter of the email body for <X>, <Y>, and <Z>.
justification_field_patterns: The prompt explicitly says '<quote>'. This rubric confirms <what>.
The prompt asks <what> and specifies <constraint>. The ground truth is <X>.
The prompt asks <X>. Splitting <Y> and <Z> into separate rubrics (instead of one bundled rubric) allows an agent that gets one item right and omits the other to receive appropriate partial credit.
This is the single hardest discriminating criterion: <why>.
Per OE<n>: <what should happen>. <Why this rubric is needed>.
forbidden_in_title: tool function names (oracle_gl_*, email_*, slack_*, sap_*, blackline_*, records_vault_*, linear_*, airtable_*, contacts_*, messaging_*, calendar_*, reminder_*)
parameter names in code-fence style (`recipient`, `channel_id`, `payload`, `content`, `body`, `kind`, `retention_policy_code` mentioned as the parameter name itself)
passive voice ('An email was sent to ...')
'at least N' (unless the prompt uses that exact phrasing for the same N)

atomicity_rules.one_independent_claim: A rubric must fail for exactly one reason. If 'sends email AND creates issue' could fail two ways, split it.
allowed_bundling: Multiple attributes of the same artifact / same tool call (recipient + CC on one send)
Multiple facts from the same data record that would always pass / fail together
forbidden_bundling: Two independent write actions in one rubric
Investigation step + write action in one rubric
Multiple distinct content items across different parameters in one rubric

outcome_v_process_balance.rule: Outcome must outnumber Process. Process > 50% of total = FAIL.
process_three_condition_test: Required by every valid solution path (phrased broadly enough to allow alternatives)
A stricter Outcome rubric cannot capture the same requirement
Describes a verification, not an execution trace
